data.title: Bank OZK sells part of defunct Chicago development for $84M
data.description:
Bank OZK (NASDAQ:OZK) closed the sale of the northern part of Chicago's failed Lincoln Yards project in Chicago to JDL Development, as expected, for $84M, the regional lender disclosed in a recent filing.

The company initially provided a $128M loan for the large Chicago land tract in 2019, but assumed ownership in March 2025 after delays and funding problems derailed prior developer Sterling Bay's plans, Bloomberg reported.

The remaining $78.6M in foreclosed assets, as of Sept. 30, 2025, comprises three Real Estate Specialties Group ("RESG") assets, the largest two of which -- land in Los Angeles and an office property in Boston -- were under contract to sell, OZK said in a quarterly Securities and Exchange Commission filing earlier in the week.

OZK's total foreclosed assets roughly halved from $160M in Q2 and ticked up from $78M in the year-ago period.

"An important element of our business model is to structure loans so that sponsors/capital partners are highly incentivized to support the loans, even in challenging times," OZK said in the 10-Q. It does so by obtaining sizable equity stakes relative to its low leverage loans and lending on strong assets, keeping sponsors and capital partners invested even if projects face delays. 

"Despite the challenging macroeconomic environment, we have had only a small number of RESG substandard credits and foreclosed assets over the past 13 quarters," OZK said.

The Thursday filing also showed that OZK's Q3 earnings rose both Q/Q and Y/Y, topping expectations, despite a second straight month of record net interest income.

OZK shares extended losses from the prior session, sliding 3.3% in Friday late morning trading, underperforming most peers intraday. The stock is on track to close out the week with an 8.5% decline, amid broader weakness across regional banks stemming from credit risk jitters.

data.title: Simmons Bank outlines 3.65%+ net interest margin target while advancing core deposit growth strategy
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Simmons First National Corporation (SFNC) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Jay Brogdon, President, described the third quarter as “an important and transformational quarter for Simmons Bank,” highlighting that the company has now addressed structural balance sheet challenges and is shifting its focus toward organic growth and operational excellence. Brogdon indicated, “We have put a lot of rigor and invested a lot of our capacity into addressing some structural challenges, the structure of our balance sheet, and we put that in the rearview window in the third quarter. And where we really shift all of our capacity and focus is really to the structure of our business.”
  • Brogdon emphasized driving “better and improved organic growth capabilities” through talent acquisition and leveraging disruption within the bank’s footprint. He noted continued efforts to “drive operational excellence in our business and gaining efficiency throughout.”
  • Brogdon underlined the company’s disciplined approach to growth, pointing to “low single-digit growth rates for loans” and a growing pipeline, while stressing that “we don’t really have to grow volume to grow net interest income right now.” He cited nearly $3 billion of fixed rate loans repricing over the next 24 months as a significant tailwind for net interest income.
  • Brogdon provided details on net interest margin (NIM), stating the September NIM was 3.76% and the fourth quarter is expected to be “at or above 3.65%.” He pointed to balance sheet restructuring and hedging as key factors supporting this outlook.
  • Charles Hobbs, Chief Financial Officer, added, “The balance sheet restructure was the biggest part of the increase in NIM, but it's also important to note that the core fundamentals that we've been seeing since the low point of Q1 of '24, which was 2.66%, we've been growing that every quarter.” Hobbs stated, “Our goal over the long term in an up or down 200, 250 basis point rate move, we'd like to keep our NIM somewhere in that 3.50% to 3.75% range.”
  • CEO George Makris commented on leadership transition, succession planning, and optimism for future opportunities, noting, “As we've said, we're well positioned to produce exceptional organic growth from this point forward. Our succession plan is firmly established, and I'm really optimistic about the future opportunities for Simmons.”

Outlook

  • Brogdon reiterated an outlook for net interest margin, stating the fourth quarter is expected to be “at or above 3.65%.” He further explained, “I think margin can kind of hold its head right here over the next 12 months. I think it can be in this area, it will, of course, move around a little bit, but I think it's very, very defensible.”
  • Hobbs indicated that the hedging program implemented in the third quarter is expected to help maintain NIM within a 3.50% to 3.75% range, even as rate cuts occur.
  • No formal loan growth target for 2026 was disclosed, with Brogdon stating, “We'll come out with an outlook, I think, more formally in January.”

Financial Results

  • Brogdon reported a September net interest margin of 3.76%. He highlighted substantial progress from the Q1 2024 low of 2.66%.
  • Hobbs discussed deposit betas, reporting a cumulative beta of 65% through the current rate cycle, and noted that this figure is expected to moderate over the next several rate cuts due to the reduction in broker deposits.
  • On the deposit side, Brogdon stated, “We're growing accounts. When we look at our consumer base, we are adding customers and growing the number of accounts in the business.” Commercial deposits and fee income have also shown growth.
  • Regarding expenses, Brogdon acknowledged the Q3 run rate of noninterest expense may be “a little above what I would really expect in Q4,” and emphasized ongoing opportunities to gain efficiency while continuing to invest in talent acquisition.

Q&A

  • David Feaster, Raymond James: Asked about strategic priorities following transformational actions. Brogdon responded that the company is focusing on organic growth, operational excellence, and disciplined risk-adjusted returns.
  • Feaster followed up on growth prospects. Brogdon clarified that growth will be pursued without sacrificing soundness or profitability, and noted tailwinds from loan repricing.
  • Feaster inquired on margin trajectory. Brogdon explained the NIM outlook and resilience, while Hobbs described the shift to asset sensitivity and the expanded hedging program.
  • Wood Lay, KBW: Asked about deposit betas and future trends. Hobbs explained the expected moderation in beta due to the removal of brokered deposits. Brogdon added that customer growth continues, but average balances per account are trending lower.
  • Lay asked about expenses. Brogdon said Q3 expenses were temporarily elevated and expects efficiency gains and ongoing talent investment.
  • Lay questioned credit quality. Brogdon responded that credit metrics were “very in-line” and reiterated ongoing efforts to resolve specific problem loans.
  • Jordan Ghent, Stephens: Asked about brokered deposits. Hobbs stated the long-term goal is to reduce brokered deposits to zero, relying on core deposit growth. Commercial deposits grew, while consumer deposits saw pressure from lower average balances.
  • Ghent asked about competitive loan pricing. Brogdon noted increased competition and “irrational” pricing from some competitors, but reaffirmed Simmons’ discipline.
  • Gary Tenner, D.A. Davidson: Inquired on deposit spot rates and CD repricing. Brogdon did not provide a spot rate but referred to disclosed margin statistics. Hobbs reported customer CDs that matured in the last 90 days had a 3.77% rate, with new CDs at 3.53%.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst tone was inquisitive and at times skeptical, particularly regarding the sustainability of margin, deposit pricing, and growth strategies. Multiple analysts pressed for clarity on competitive dynamics and future targets.
  • Management maintained a confident and measured tone during prepared remarks, frequently emphasizing discipline, defensibility of margins, and optimism for future growth. In the Q&A, management continued to stress discipline and operational excellence, occasionally acknowledging challenges from competition and rate environment.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone was more assertive about having completed transformational actions and being well positioned for organic growth. Analyst sentiment was slightly more probing this quarter, particularly around the implications of recent restructuring.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter featured a significant strategic shift with balance sheet restructuring, completion of major internal changes, and a leadership transition. Last quarter focused more on gradual performance improvement and remixing of deposits.
  • Current quarter guidance provided a specific NIM target for Q4 and the next 12 months, while last quarter’s outlook was less specific.
  • Management’s tone was notably more confident about defending margins and pursuing growth, highlighting the transition from structural correction to growth focus. Analyst questions this quarter reflected greater scrutiny of the sustainability and execution of these changes.
  • Key metrics such as net interest margin showed marked improvement compared to the previous quarter, with management referencing a September NIM of 3.76% versus a Q1 2024 low of 2.66%.
  • The competitive environment and pricing discipline were discussed in both quarters, but this quarter included explicit examples of competitive behavior impacting loan pricing decisions.

Risks and Concerns

  • Competition in loan pricing remains a major headwind, with Brogdon referencing “irrational” pricing in the market.
  • Deposit growth continues, but is challenged by declining average account balances on the consumer side.
  • Management identified the need to resolve a small number of problem loans, with ongoing efforts to migrate these out of the portfolio.
  • Expense control remains a focus, balanced against continued investment in talent and operational efficiency.

Final Takeaway

Simmons First National Corporation’s third quarter 2025 call underscored the completion of major balance sheet actions and a renewed focus on organic growth, efficiency, and margin stability. Management emphasized a disciplined approach to growth, supported by repricing tailwinds, hedging strategies, and ongoing talent investment. The company’s outlook projects a defensible net interest margin at or above 3.65% for the coming quarter and stability over the next year, while acknowledging ongoing challenges from competitive loan pricing and consumer deposit trends. The transition in leadership was framed as a continuation of this strategic direction, with management expressing optimism about Simmons’ positioning for future performance.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: OpenAI pauses Sora's AI video generations of MLK, Jr., following distasteful depictions
data.description:
OpenAI (OPENAI) has halted the usage of Martin Luther King, Jr.'s image on its artificial intelligence video generation app Sora, following "disrespectful depictions" the Microsoft-backed (NASDAQ:MSFT) company announced. 

"The Estate of Martin Luther King, Jr., Inc. and OpenAI have worked together to address how Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.'s likeness is represented in Sora generations. Some users generated disrespectful depictions of Dr. King's image," OpenAI wrote in a post on X. "So at King, Inc.'s request, OpenAI has paused generations depicting Dr. King as it strengthens guardrails for historical figures."

The Sora app allows users to transform text prompts and images into realistic short videos. These can then be shared with friends and other users in the app.

"While there are strong free speech interests in depicting historical figures, OpenAI believes public figures and their families should ultimately have control over how their likeness is used," the company said. "Authorized representatives or estate owners can request that their likeness not be used in Sora cameos."

The Sora app currently ranks as the No. 1 free download in the Apple (AAPL) App Store. It is followed by OpenAI's ChatGPT at No. 2 and Google's (GOOG)(GOOGL) Gemini at No. 3. The app is not yet available on Android, so it has not arrived on Google Play, but it is available for pre-registration in the U.S. and Canada.

The app was downloaded more than 1M times within the first five days of its launch in late September, despite initially being available by invite only and just in North American markets.

data.title: Hims & Hers Health down 11% after CEO Dudum stock sale disclosure
data.description:
• Shares of Hims & Hers Health (NYSE:HIMS) are down ~11% Friday following a Thursday afternoon disclosure that CEO Andrew Dudum had sold ~$11M worth of stock that day.
  • An SEC filing stated that the sale of 175,661 shares was completed under Rule 144, which involves the sale of restricted and controlled securities. It appears that the trade was done under a predetermined investment plan.
  • About 125,000 shares were from options, while the rest was through restricted stock vesting.
  • The SEC filing also indicates that from mid-July through mid-September, Dudum or one of his trusts sold ~$29.3M worth of shares.

data.title: Hong Kong's ACCO Group prices IPO at $4/shr, offering 1.4 million shares
data.description:
• ACCO Group Holdings (NASDAQ:ACCL) announced Friday the pricing of its initial public offering, offering 1.4 million ordinary shares at $4.00 per share.
  • The ordinary shares are expected to commence trading on the Nasdaq Capital Market on October 17, 2025, under the ticker symbol “ACCL.”
  • The offering is expected to close on October 20, and the company intends to use the net proceeds for expanding the corporate service business, incorporating generative artificial intelligence features into the business modules, and building up the presence in the United States market.

data.title: I-Mab tumbles after announcing new business model
data.description:
I-Mab (NASDAQ:IMAB) shares fell ~14% on Friday after the cancer drug developer announced a new “hub-and-spoke” business model, under which it plans to seek a name change and a potential dual listing on Nasdaq and Hong Kong.

Concurrently, the company said that Kyler Lei, a former deputy general manager and head of capital markets at Sino Biopharmaceutical Limited, will be its new CFO effective Oct. 16, replacing Joseph Skelton.

As part of the new business model, the Rockville, Maryland-based biotech aims to establish and develop specialized subsidiary units (spokes) that would focus on a specific asset or therapeutic area.

Under the new business plan, I-Mab (NASDAQ:IMAB) has created Visara, a new subsidiary through which it aims to acquire VIS-101, a bifunctional biologic currently undergoing Phase 2 development, within this month.

Following the model change, the company also intends to rebrand itself as NovaBridge Biosciences on Oct. 16, subject to shareholder approval it plans to receive at an Extraordinary General Meeting scheduled for Oct. 24.

As for dual listing plans, I-Mab (NASDAQ:IMAB) seeks to pursue a Hong Kong IPO, the terms of which remain undecided and will be subject to market conditions and regulatory approvals, the company said.

data.title: Five Below opens stores in Pacific Northwest
data.description:
• Five Below (NASDAQ:FIVE) is expanding into the Pacific Northwest with eight new stores in Washington and Oregon.
  • All eight stores will open on November 7 with promotions such as free giveaways, exclusive deals, and games with prizes.
  • The newest store openings bring the discount chain’s current footprint to more than 1,850 stores in 46 states.

data.title: Renewal Fuels jumps on OTC compliance, governance improvements
data.description:
Renewal Fuels (OTC:RNWF) on Friday said that it has achieved full compliance with all OTC Markets reporting requirements and has implemented significant governance and capital structure improvements, fueling gains in morning trading.

The company maintains "zero toxic debt financing, providing operational flexibility and shareholder protection."

Additionally, the cancellation process is underway for approximately 1.683 billion shares, which, upon completion, will reduce the total issued and outstanding share count to approximately 1.115 billion shares.

"This restructuring represents a fundamental reset that provides Renewal Fuels with the clean corporate foundation necessary to pursue transformational growth opportunities," said Richard Hawkins, president and CEO of Renewal Fuels.

Renewal Fuels (OTC:RNWF) was up 66% at the time of writing.

data.title: Bristol Myers Squibb, SystImmune report data from lung cancer trial
data.description:
Bristol Myers Squibb (NYSE:BMY) and SystImmune reported data from an early-stage study testing their bispecific antibody-drug conjugate, iza-bren, as a treatment for advanced non-small cell lung cancer and other solid tumors.

Iza-bren is developed collaboratively by SystImmune and Bristol Myers Squibb, with an exclusive license for regions outside Mainland China.

The trial results indicate that iza-bren shows antitumor effects in patients who have been heavily treated for various tumor types.

The study also reported a manageable safety profile, with any hematologic side effects being addressed through standard medical interventions, and no cases of interstitial lung disease were noted.

data.title: Wall Street cautiously advances as investors navigate worries on regional banks' health
data.description:
U.S. stocks were higher on Friday, as Wall Street was rattled by concerns of the health of regional banks and credit risks while the U.S. government shutdown entered its 17th day.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.3% in late morning trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.3%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.4%.

“Markets are now paying attention to credit, and there are likely more skeletons in the closet of lenders. I am not sure yet whether this poses systemic risk, but the gaping jaw between business development companies and the S&P 500 will close one way or another,” said Chester Ntonifor, chief strategist at BCA Research, via X.com.

Stocks of investment bank Jefferies Financial (JEF) +6.3%, regional banks Zions Bancorporation (ZION) +5.4%, and Western Alliance (WAL) +3.3% were initially lower on Friday as investors worried about the quality of loans and investments made by the firms, but have since recovered. Goldman Sachs (GS) and J.P. Morgan (JPM), however, are some of the top decliners in the Dow (DJI).

The KBW Nasdaq Regional Banking Index (KRX) closed down 6.3% on Thursday trading, its biggest drop since April, following the disclosures by two banks of irregularities related to some loans -- last 1.3%.

Meanwhile, the U.S. government shutdown continued to grind on as it entered its third week, after a GOP funding bill failed for the 10th time. Prediction market Kalshi now sees the government shutdown lasting almost 40 days, the longest in history.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 4 basis points to 4.02%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 4 basis points to 3.47%.

“The bond market is pricing in 2 more 25 bps rate cuts by year-end and 3 more 25 bps cuts in 2026. That would bring the Fed Funds Rate below 3%. After a brief hiatus, easy money is back,” said Charlie Bilello, chief market strategist at Creative Planning.

Wall Street's major averages closed lower on Thursday amid increased volatility due to the ongoing U.S.-China trade conflict and bad loan reports in banks, while investors continued to assess earnings reports and the ongoing U.S. government shutdown.
